basic_details.job_title: Delhi State Cancer Institutes Junior Residents Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for 16 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Delhi State Cancer Institutes (DSCI) Junior Residents Notification 2026 has been released on the official website dsci.nic.in. A total of 16 vacancies have been announced through the DSCI Junior Residents Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age: 40 years Age and Experience etc. can be relaxed at the discretion of the Selection Board/Governing Council of DSCI for the candidates otherwise will be qualified and well conversant with their field or for those belonging to reserved category as per Govt Rules.

qualification.education_qualification: MBBS from a recognized university. Must have completed one year internship not before 2 years as on date of interview and enrolled with Delhi Medical Council., The candidate must not have completed 01-year Junior Residency in any Govt hospital in India., FMGE Certificate for FMG candidates in eligibility qualification / required document list.
qualification.details: MBBS from a recognized university. Must have completed one year internship not before 2 years as on date of interview and enrolled with Delhi Medical Council. The candidate must not have completed 01-year Junior Residency in any Govt hospital in India. FMGE Certificate for FMG candidates in eligibility qualification / required document list.

selection_process: Walk-in Interview Written Exam (if large number of candidates)

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to attend the walk-in interview for Delhi State Cancer Institutes Junior Residents Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview is scheduled for 25th May 2026. Candidates should report to the venue on this date with all necessary documents.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for the Junior Residents post?
faq.answer: There are a total of 16 vacancies for the Junior Residents post in the Delhi State Cancer Institutes Recruitment 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for the Junior Residents post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess an MBBS degree from a recognized university and have completed one year of internship. They should also be enrolled with the Delhi Medical Council.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the Junior Residents post?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Junior Residents post is 40 years. Age relaxation may be applicable as per government rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Junior Residents position?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will receive a salary of Rs. 56,100 per month, plus usual allowances as per pay matrix level 10.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q7. What is the mode of application for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The mode of application is Walk-in. Candidates need to attend the interview directly at the specified venue and time.

faq.question: Q8. What documents should I carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry all original documents, one set of self-attested photocopies, ID proof, educational certificates, experience certificates, and category certificates (if applicable).

faq.question: Q9. Where is the Delhi State Cancer Institutes located?
faq.answer: The Delhi State Cancer Institutes is located in Delhi, with a pincode of 110095.

faq.question: Q10. What is the selection process for the Junior Residents post?
faq.answer: The selection process includes a Walk-in Interview, and a Written Exam may be conducted if there is a large number of candidates.
